description_long: This is a rectangular, L-shaped bench with a stepped seating surface, constructed primarily from what appears to be wood or a wood-like material in a reddish-brown color. The bench is supported by six straight legs, likely made of metal, with additional cross-bracing for stability visible on the longer side. The seat surface is composed of wide, evenly spaced planks, and the bench lacks both a backrest and armrests, making it suitable for sitting from either side. The design is modern and utilitarian, with clean lines and a robust structure, suggesting it is intended for public or communal use. The stepped section on one end provides a unique visual element and may serve as a functional divider or accommodate different seating heights. This bench would be well-suited for placement in parks, sidewalks, or other urban outdoor environments.
description: A modern, L-shaped reddish-brown bench with a stepped seat and six straight metal legs, featuring cross-bracing for stability. The design is robust and backless, ideal for outdoor public spaces.
description_view_0: Front angled view showing the L-shaped stepped seat, six legs, and the cross-bracing beneath the bench.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile and the stepped section on the left end of the bench.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full extent of the cross-bracing and the continuous plank seat surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the long, uninterrupted seat and the stepped section on the right.
attribute_leg_design: six straight metal legs with cross-bracing
